# QA Tracking Report — Unified Fix Plan

**Created:** 2026-03-12  
**Sources:** [March 10 internal QA report](./qa-report-2026-03-10.md) + March 11 GPT first-time-user QA report (attached to GitHub issue)  
**Branch:** `copilot/handle-quirks-and-issues`  
**Purpose:** Living document — tracks every identified issue, its priority, the fix plan, and current status. Update the Status column as each sub-PR is merged.

> **Status key:** 🔴 Open · 🟡 In Progress · ✅ Fixed · ⚠️ Partially Fixed · 🚫 Deferred

---

## Executive Summary

Two QA sessions (one automated internal session on March 10, one first-time-user GPT session on March 11) were merged into this single tracking document. Combined findings: **1 High**, **5 Medium**, **9 Low** severity bugs, plus **2 features/investigations** surfaced by the first-time-user session.

### What was working well (both reports agreed)

- Help documentation is clear and comprehensive
- Custom team creation, validation, and random generation are strong
- Automated gameplay flow and Manager Mode prompts feel polished
- Export/import of teams and saves works end-to-end
- Drag-and-drop lineup/bench reordering works correctly

### Top problems (combined)

| Rank | Issue | Severity | Source |
| ---- | ----- | -------- | ------ |
| 1 | Loading a save for a deleted team shows raw team ID in tabs and hit log | High | March 10 |
| 2 | Hit log shows `#N` slot number instead of player name | Medium | March 10 |
| 3 | `useRxdbGameSync: failed to update progress (game over)` console error | Medium | March 10 |
| 4 | "Resume Current Game" shown after a FINAL game | Medium | March 10 + March 11 |
| 5 | Save import error leaks the word "undefined" | Medium | March 10 |
| 6 | No starter / demo teams — new users cannot play immediately | High UX | March 11 |
| 7 | Load Saved Game dedicated page showed empty even when saves exist | Medium UX | March 11 |
| 8 | Career Stats page empty after completing a game | Medium UX | March 11 |

---

## Issue Register

### Sub-PR 1 — Group 2: Bugs A + J *(ship together — same rendering area)*

| ID | Severity | Title | Source | Status |
| -- | -------- | ----- | ------ | ------ |
| Bug J | 🔴 High | Loading a save for a deleted team shows raw team ID in tabs and hit log | March 10 | 🟡 In Progress |
| Bug A | 🔵 Medium | Hit log shows `#N` position number instead of player name | March 10 | 🟡 In Progress |

**Files changed:**
- `src/features/gameplay/context/index.tsx` — add `batterName?: string` to `PlayLogEntry`
- `src/features/gameplay/context/hitBall.ts` — populate `batterName` from active lineup at time of hit
- `src/features/gameplay/components/HitLog/index.tsx` — use `teamLabels[entry.team]` instead of `resolveTeamLabel` (fixes Bug J); render `entry.batterName ?? \`#${entry.batterNum}\`` (fixes Bug A)

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Hit log entries show batter name (e.g. `"▲3 — Roberto Martinez"`), not `#4`
- [ ] Backward-compatible: older saves without `batterName` fall back to `#N`
- [ ] Loading a save for a deleted team shows the original team name (from `teamLabels`), not a raw ID

---

### Sub-PR 2 — Bug C + Bug 3 *(related: both concern the game-over state)*

| ID | Severity | Title | Source | Status |
| -- | -------- | ----- | ------ | ------ |
| Bug C | 🔵 Medium | "Resume Current Game" shown on home screen after a FINAL game | March 10 + March 11 | 🟡 In Progress |
| Bug 3 | 🔵 Medium | `useRxdbGameSync: failed to update progress (game over)` console error | March 10 | 🟡 In Progress |

**Files changed (Bug C):**
- `src/storage/types.ts` — add `onGameOver: () => void` to `AppShellOutletContext`
- `src/features/gameplay/components/AppShell/index.tsx` — add `handleGameOver` callback that resets `hasActiveSession` to `false`; expose via outlet context
- `src/features/gameplay/components/Game/GameInner.tsx` — call `onGameOver` prop when `gameOver` transitions to `true`
- `src/features/gameplay/pages/GamePage/index.tsx` — thread `onGameOver` from outlet context down to `<Game>`

**Files changed (Bug 3):**
- `src/features/saves/hooks/useRxdbGameSync.ts` — accept `wasAlreadyFinalOnLoad` param; guard game-over `updateProgress` call when `wasAlreadyFinalOnLoad === true`
- `src/features/saves/storage/saveStore.ts` — make `updateProgress` silently resolve (not throw) when the target save no longer exists

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] After a game reaches FINAL naturally, the home screen does NOT show a Resume button
- [ ] After loading a FINAL save and navigating home, no Resume button
- [ ] For an in-progress game, Resume button still appears and works
- [ ] No console error fires when a game ends naturally
- [ ] No console error fires when loading a FINAL save and navigating home
- [ ] No console error fires when clicking Resume for a FINAL game

---

### Sub-PR 3 — Group 3: Bugs F + 5 *(ship together — same "undefined" error category)*

| ID | Severity | Title | Source | Status |
| -- | -------- | ----- | ------ | ------ |
| Bug F | 🔵 Medium | Save import error leaks the word "undefined" | March 10 | 🟡 In Progress |
| Bug 5 | 🟡 Low | Team import error leaks the word "undefined" | March 10 | 🟡 In Progress |

**Files changed:**
- `src/features/saves/storage/saveStore.ts` — guard `version` type before comparing; emit user-friendly message when `version` is absent or not a number
- `src/features/customTeams/storage/customTeamExportImport.ts` — guard `formatVersion` type before comparing; emit user-friendly message with no raw tokens

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Pasting `{}` into save import shows a clean message with no `undefined`/`null`/`NaN`
- [ ] Pasting `{}` into team import shows a clean message with no `undefined`/`null`/`NaN`
- [ ] Valid imports still succeed without errors

### Sub-PR 4 — Bugs B + G *(standalone low-risk fixes)*

| ID | Severity | Title | Source | Status |
| -- | -------- | ----- | ------ | ------ |
| Bug B | 🟡 Low | Save deletion has no confirmation dialog | March 10 | 🟡 In Progress |
| Bug G | 🟡 Low | ← Back on player career page uses browser history, not `/stats` | March 10 | 🟡 In Progress |

**Files changed:**
- `src/features/saves/components/SaveSlotList/index.tsx` — wrap `onDelete` call in `window.confirm()`, matching the pattern used for team deletion
- `src/features/careerStats/pages/PlayerCareerPage/index.tsx` — replace `navigate(-1)` with explicit `navigate(teamParam ? \`/stats?team=${...}\` : "/stats")`

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] Clicking ✕ on a save shows a confirmation prompt before deleting
- [ ] Clicking ← Back from any player career page always lands on `/stats`, not a previous player

### Sub-PR 6 — March 11 Priority 1: Demo / starter teams

| ID | Severity | Title | Source | Status |
| -- | -------- | ----- | ------ | ------ |
| New-1 | 🔴 High UX | No starter teams — new users cannot play immediately | March 11 | 🟡 In Progress |

**Context:** When a brand-new user visits the app, they must create at least two custom teams before they can play. This is the single biggest first-time-user friction point identified in the March 11 report.

**Files changed:**
- `src/features/customTeams/generation/demoTeams.ts` *(new)* — two fully-populated hardcoded team definitions with real-looking names, cities, and stat distributions
- `src/shared/hooks/useSeedDemoTeams.ts` *(new)* — hook: on first load, if `customTeams` collection is empty, inserts the two demo teams via `CustomTeamStore.createCustomTeam`
- `src/features/gameplay/components/AppShell/index.tsx` — call `useSeedDemoTeams()` so the check runs once on app start

**Acceptance criteria:**
- [ ] A brand-new user (empty IndexedDB) sees two teams already present when they open Manage Teams
- [ ] New Game works immediately for a brand-new user without any manual team creation
- [ ] Users who already have custom teams see no change (hook is a no-op when teams exist)
- [ ] Demo teams are fully valid (pass all schema/stat validation)

---

## Investigations (March 11 report — not confirmed code bugs)

### Inv-1: Load Saved Game page shows empty when saves exist in the in-game modal

**Status:** 🔴 Needs investigation  
**Context:** The March 11 tester found the `/saves` page showed "No saves yet" even though saves were visible in the in-game 💾 modal. Both surfaces use the same underlying `getDb()` singleton and should see identical data. The most likely explanations are a test-environment artifact (different browser session) or a timing issue on first navigation to `/saves` before DB is ready.  
**Action:** Verify reproducibility. If confirmed: investigate whether `getDb()` can return a different instance than what RxDatabaseProvider uses inside the game, or whether there is a timing window during first initialization. If not reproducible: document as environment-specific and close.

### Inv-2: Career Stats page empty after completing a full game

**Status:** 🔴 Needs investigation  
**Context:** The March 11 tester saw "No completed games yet for this team" on the `/stats` page after playing a full exhibition game. The in-game Career tab (computed from the live play log) showed stats correctly. `useGameHistorySync` should commit stats to RxDB when `gameOver` becomes true, using `gameInstanceId` as the primary key.  
**Action:** Verify reproducibility. If confirmed: check whether `gameOver` is true at commit time, whether `gameInstanceId` is populated, and whether `commitCompletedGame` is throwing silently. Add explicit error logging if needed. Note: Bug 3 (wave 2) fixes a console error that fires after game-over, and the `wasAlreadyFinalOnLoad` fix may also affect this flow — re-test career stats after Sub-PR 2 is merged.

---

## Bug 1 — Deferred (needs verification before implementing)

| ID | Severity | Title | Source | Status |
| -- | -------- | ----- | ------ | ------ |
| Bug 1 | 🔵 Medium | Player career page shows raw ID for bench players with no appearances | March 10 | 🚫 Deferred — needs reproduction |

**Context:** Root cause is understood but the bug was not directly reproduced in either QA session. Requires a custom-team bench player who has zero game appearances, accessed via a URL without the `?team=` query param.  
**Before implementing:** Manually reproduce as described in [bug-01-bench-player-raw-id.md](./qa-2026-03-10/bug-01-bench-player-raw-id.md).

## Implementation order

Work top-to-bottom. Each sub-PR is independent unless noted.

| Sub-PR | Bugs | Priority | Parallel-safe? |
| ------ | ---- | -------- | -------------- |
| 1 — Group 2 | A + J | Ship first (High + Medium) | ✅ Yes |
| 2 — Bug C + Bug 3 | C + 3 | Ship second (Medium × 2) | ✅ Yes (no shared files with Sub-PR 1) |
| 3 — Group 3 | F + 5 | Ship third (after Sub-PR 2 — both touch `saveStore.ts`) | ⚠️ Do not run in parallel with Sub-PR 2 |
| 4 — Bugs B + G | B + G | Any time after Sub-PR 1 | ✅ Yes |
| 5 — Group 1 | D + E + H + I | Any time | ✅ Yes |
| 6 — Demo teams | New-1 | Any time (independent) | ✅ Yes |
